text:
```python
import math

xs = [4, 8, 15, 16, 23, 42]
mean = sum(xs) / len(xs)
variance = sum((x - mean) ** 2 for x in xs) / len(xs)
std_dev = math.sqrt(variance)

print(f"{std_dev:.2f}")
```

Hint: The formula for population standard deviation involves the square root of the average of the squared differences from the mean.
